evals.prompt: Set up JWT authentication for my FastAPI app. Access tokens should expire after 15 minutes and I need a refresh token mechanism.
evals.expected_output: Agent implements create_access_token() and decode_token() using python-jose, ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL=15min, get_current_user Depends() injector. Implements refresh token: stored hashed in DB, httpOnly cookie, rotated on use, full token family revocation on reuse detection.

evals.prompt: Do a security review of my user profile endpoint: GET /api/v1/users/{user_id}. Any user can call it with any user_id.
evals.expected_output: Agent identifies IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) vulnerability - A01 Broken Access Control. Proposes fix: add row-level check (order.user_id == current_user.id), return 404 (not 403) for unauthorized access to hide resource existence. Also checks for missing auth dependency and response model leaking internal fields.

evals.prompt: I want to add file upload support. Users can upload profile pictures.
evals.expected_output: Agent specifies: validate MIME type server-side (not just extension), enforce 5MB max size, store in S3 not local disk (with pre-signed URL for access), sanitize filename (uuid-based, not user-supplied), scan for malicious content via ClamAV or similar. OWASP file upload risks cited.
